add_changelog_entry.py.description: Append a factual shipped-change entry to CHANGELOG.json or CHANGELOG.md.

input.rule: Pass one concise summary string or a comma-separated list string; avoid shell-specific array conventions.

output.success: Appends one changelog entry and prints the target feature or timestamp.
output.failure: Throws when the changelog path is missing, the timestamp is invalid, or Summary is empty.

audit_docs.py.description: Python-first document audit entrypoint for JSON document contracts and smoke sweeps.

input.rule: Run from the repository root or pass --repo-root. This is the Python smoke entrypoint; broader legacy audit parity remains tracked as migration debt until all legacy-shell-only checks are ported.

output.success: Runs the Python JSON document audit and prints OK when document contracts pass.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when JSON documents, generated document cache, or checked template schema rules fail.

audit_json_docs.py.description: Validate JSON document files for parseability and repository document conventions.

input.rule: Run from the repository root and prefer wrapper scripts over internal engines for normal document edits.

output.success: Completes the document operation or check and prints the requested result or OK status.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when paths, selectors, duplicate policy, schema rules, normalization, or post-edit checks fail.

check_code_context_gate.py.description: Verify that required compiled code-guidance context was loaded for the current HEAD before code-changing work.

input.rule: Run from the repository root when verifying codebase context or generated codebase indexes.

output.success: Completes the codebase verification or reporting step and exits 0 when the contract is satisfied.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when required codebase context or generated codebase artifacts are missing or stale.

check_ai_compiled_context.py.description: Python cheap check for compiled-context anchor files and bootstrap-index freshness.

input.rule: Use for Python-first smoke sweeps when full compiled-context regeneration is not being requested.

output.success: Prints OK when required compiled-context anchors exist and the bootstrap index is current.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when compiled-context anchors are missing or the bootstrap index is stale.

check_evals.py.description: Python-first checker for manual eval framework files, prompt scenarios, personas, expected behaviors, scoring docs, and result JSONs.

input.rule: Run from the repository root or pass --repo-root. Use --eval-dir only when checking a nonstandard eval directory.

output.success: Prints OK when required eval files parse and scenario references are consistent.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when eval files are missing, invalid JSON, or internally inconsistent.

input.rule: Use documented script parameters for the selected workflow.

output.success: Exits 0 and prints status when the operation succeeds.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero with a concrete error.

check_pipeline_feature_contracts.py.description: Python-first validator for pipeline_featurelist.json feature artifact contracts and changed pipeline-owned path coverage.

input.rule: Use this Python entrypoint for source and installed-package feature contract checks. Pass changed paths for focused task verification; omit paths for a full feature contract audit.

output.success: Prints the number of covered feature contracts and confirms changed pipeline paths are assigned.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when feature contracts, required artifacts, catalog entries, managed-package coverage, or changed-path coverage fail.

check_sensitive_reference_leaks.py.description: Python-first smoke-test for private project, private domain, and private identity references before publication or pipeline sync.

input.rule: Run without arguments from the source pipeline repository; pass --repo-root only when checking another checkout.

output.success: Exits 0 when no blocked private references are found.
output.failure: Exits nonzero and prints file, line, rule, and matched text for every blocked reference up to max findings.

check_python_syntax.py.description: Check Python source syntax in memory without writing bytecode cache files.

input.rule: Use this instead of py_compile for Python-first quality gates that must not create __pycache__ artifacts.

output.success: Prints OK with the number of checked Python files.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when a source file is missing or cannot compile.

check_runtime.py.description: Report required Python runtime availability as JSON for setup, update, and degraded-mode decisions.

input.rule: Run through the current Python runtime when setup or update needs toolchain status; set SOCRATEX_PYTHON to override discovery.

output.success: Prints a JSON object with Python availability, version, executable, source, minimum/recommended version, and install hints.
output.failure: With --strict, exits nonzero when Python 3.10+ cannot be resolved.

script_runtime_gate.py.description: Python gate that fails while tracked automation still contains retired script files or canonical references to the retired command runtime.

input.rule: Run from the repository root or pass --repo-root. Do not pass --skip-part for final verification; it exists only for local exploratory slicing.

output.success: Exits 0 and reports pass when no retired script files or canonical retired-runtime references remain.
output.failure: Exits nonzero and reports counts, top-level ownership groups, blocked script files, and reference locations.

python_runtime.py.description: Resolve and report the required SocratexPipeline Python runtime without depending on legacy shell.

input.rule: Use before Python-first pipeline tools when runtime availability is uncertain. Set SOCRATEX_PYTHON to override discovery.

output.success: Prints JSON runtime status with executable, version, source, minimum version, and install hint fields.
output.failure: With --strict, exits nonzero when Python 3.10+ cannot be resolved.

pipeline_package.py.description: Shared managed-package constants for Python-first update and feature-contract tooling.

input.rule: Internal helper imported by Python pipeline tools; not normally invoked directly.

output.success: Provides default managed package, generated, and protected path constants to Python callers.
output.failure: Import fails when the source package is missing or Python cannot read the file.

ai_native_contract_dry_run.py.description: Print the AI-native code contract header standard and list likely source files that should receive it in later per-system rollout passes. Dry-run only; it never edits source files.

input.rule: Run from a project root or pass -ProjectRoot <path>. Use the output as planning/audit evidence, not as an automatic rewrite.

output.success: Prints the JSON-like comment header standard and a scored candidate file list.
output.failure: Throws when ProjectRoot cannot be resolved.

check_task.py.description: Run the compact repository task check: code-context gate, normalization, bootstrap, diff, UTF-8, feature-list guard, line index, and optional docs audit.

input.rule: Use before committing changed source-pipeline files; pass -Audit for broad or document-affecting changes.

output.success: Refreshes/checks selected artifacts and prints OK: task check completed.
output.failure: Throws when any selected gate, normalizer check, bootstrap check, audit, or git check fails.

check_utf8_writes.py.description: Scan repository text writes for unsafe encoding or newline drift.

input.rule: Run from the repository root when checking or normalizing repository text files.

output.success: Completes text normalization, repair, write, or check work and prints OK or the generated output.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when files cannot be read/written or check mode finds stale normalization.

legacy_commit_task_compatibility_wrapper.description: Compatibility wrapper that forwards legacy commit-task calls to finalize_changed_files_commit_push.py.

input.rule: Use only for older automation; new flows should call finalize_changed_files_commit_push.py or finalize_task_check_commit_push.py directly.

output.success: Delegates to the modern finalizer and exits 0 when it succeeds.
output.failure: Throws when delegation or the underlying finalizer fails.

compile_pipeline_context.py.description: Python orchestration entrypoint for refreshing or checking pipeline bootstrap and AI-compiled context.

input.rule: Use this as the Python-first replacement for compile_pipeline_context.py during Pass 0S.

output.success: Refreshes/checks pipeline bootstrap and AI-compiled context through Python tools.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when bootstrap, compiled context generation, or compiled context check fails.

generate_installed_agent_instructions.py.description: Generate installed-project AGENTS.md instructions for selected Socratex pipeline packs.

input.rule: Run from the repository root or an explicit target path when initializing, syncing, compiling, or migrating pipeline packages.

output.success: Completes the pipeline operation and writes, checks, or prints the requested pipeline artifact.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when inputs are invalid, target paths are unsafe, generated artifacts are stale, or pipeline checks fail.

console_summary.py.description: Summarize the current console log into a compact diagnostics artifact.

input.rule: Run from the repository root when producing diagnostics artifacts from logs or console output.

output.success: Writes or prints the requested diagnostics summary.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when log inputs are missing, unreadable, or summary writing fails.

context_tags.py.description: Extract normalized context tags from task text for knowledge selection and routing with Python-only tooling.

input.rule: Run from the repository root before substantive answers or command execution when routing through compiled knowledge context.

output.success: Completes the knowledge operation and prints the selected output, report, or OK status.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when source knowledge, compiled artifacts, filters, tiers, or mutation targets are invalid.

detect_project_stack.py.description: Detect the target project stack and emit setup hints for pipeline initialization.

input.rule: Run from the repository root or setup target when checking host/runtime setup.

output.success: Completes setup probing or configuration and prints the resulting setup status.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when runtime detection or requested setup configuration fails.

build_document_cache.py.description: Build the structured document read cache through Python with a Python entrypoint.

output.success: Writes docs-tech/cache/doc_index.json or the configured output and prints the output path.

finalize_task_check_commit_push.py.description: Preferred Git-backed task finalizer: run checks including quality, commit, and push.

input.rule: Use for normal task closure when changed files should be committed and pushed. It delegates to finalize_changed_files_commit_push.py with quality enabled. Pass -RequireTaskFlowEvidence with -TaskFlowEvidencePath to hard-gate closure on artifact evidence JSON.

output.success: Checks pass, intentional paths are committed, push runs unless disabled, and final repository state is reported.
output.failure: Throws when checks, staging, commit, push, or clean-tree verification fail.

input.rule: Use during Pass 0S as the Python replacement for finalizer OUTPUT snapshot generation.

output.success: Writes the closure snapshot and reports the output path.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when repository inspection or output writing fails.

finalize_changed_files_commit_push.py.description: Python-first Git-derived changed-file finisher for source-pipeline task closure.

input.rule: Use during Pass 0S to close source-pipeline tasks through Python final checks, git staging, commit, and optional push.

output.success: Runs Python-backed final checks, stages git-derived non-local-artifact paths, commits, pushes unless disabled, and reports remaining status.
output.failure: Exits nonzero on pre-staged changes unless allowed, empty candidate set, failed checks, failed git operations, or dirty final state.

run_final_task_checks.py.description: Python-first no-commit final verification path for checks that already have Python equivalents.

input.rule: Use during Pass 0S as the Python closure path for check-only verification. It intentionally does not refresh generated artifacts until Python generators exist for every required refresh.

output.success: Runs Python-backed closure checks and exits 0 after OK.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when git diff check, feature contracts, compiled-context anchors, docs audit, quality gate, or Python cache check fails.

knowledge_file_rename.py.description: Rename a knowledge source path in JSON file-fallback knowledge tables with Python-only tooling.

input.rule: Run from the repository root using the Python wrapper.

output.success: Completes the knowledge operation and prints the selected output, report, or OK status.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when source knowledge, compiled artifacts, filters, tiers, or mutation targets are invalid.

knowledge_file_select.py.description: Select formatted knowledge context from JSON file-fallback tables with Python-only tooling.

input.rule: Run from the repository root after source knowledge changes or when selecting compiled knowledge context using the Python wrapper.

output.success: Completes the knowledge operation and prints the selected output, report, or OK status.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when source knowledge, compiled artifacts, filters, tiers, or mutation targets are invalid.

knowledge_file_upsert.py.description: Upsert source documents into JSON file-fallback knowledge tables with Python-only tooling.

input.rule: Run from the repository root using the Python wrapper.

output.success: Completes the knowledge operation and prints the selected output, report, or OK status.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when source knowledge, compiled artifacts, filters, tiers, or mutation targets are invalid.

knowledge_index.py.description: Internal engine for compiling, querying, selecting, renaming, and mutating knowledge indexes.

input.rule: Run from the repository root after source knowledge changes or when selecting compiled knowledge context.

output.success: Completes the knowledge operation and prints the selected output, report, or OK status.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when source knowledge, compiled artifacts, filters, tiers, or mutation targets are invalid.

knowledge_tier_report.py.description: Report source knowledge documentation entries grouped by context_tier, categories/tags, type, and document path.

input.rule: Use for tier audits and category summaries; add -Strict to turn missing or invalid tiers into a failing check.

output.success: Prints markdown or JSON summary with counts by tier, category, and explicit tier coverage status.
output.failure: With -Strict, exits nonzero when missing/invalid context_tier entries or JSON parse errors are found.

knowledge_tier_check.py.description: Python-first strict checker for context_tier metadata on knowledge documentation entries.

input.rule: Run after knowledge tier migrations or before relying on tier-scoped context routing. Include templates when starter/template knowledge docs are in scope.

output.success: Prints a tier coverage report and exits 0 when every discovered knowledge entry has a valid context_tier.
output.failure: Exits nonzero and lists missing or invalid context_tier entries or parse errors.

knowledge_query.py.description: Query the SQLite knowledge database with Python-only tooling.

input.rule: Run from the repository root using the Python wrapper.

output.success: Completes the knowledge operation and prints the selected output, report, or OK status.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when source knowledge, compiled artifacts, filters, tiers, or mutation targets are invalid.

knowledge_rename.py.description: Rename a knowledge source path in the SQLite database with Python-only tooling.

input.rule: Run from the repository root using the Python wrapper.

output.success: Completes the knowledge operation and prints the selected output, report, or OK status.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when source knowledge, compiled artifacts, filters, tiers, or mutation targets are invalid.

knowledge_select.py.description: Select formatted knowledge context from compiled SQLite knowledge or file fallback with Python-only tooling.

input.rule: Run from the repository root when a named view, tag query, type filter, source document, or startup flag can answer the task.

output.success: Completes the knowledge operation and prints the selected output, report, or OK status.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when source knowledge, compiled artifacts, filters, tiers, or mutation targets are invalid.

knowledge_upsert.py.description: Upsert source documents into the SQLite knowledge database with Python-only tooling.

input.rule: Run from the repository root using the Python wrapper.

output.success: Completes the knowledge operation and prints the selected output, report, or OK status.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when source knowledge, compiled artifacts, filters, tiers, or mutation targets are invalid.

input.rule: Use documented script parameters for the selected workflow.

output.success: Exits 0 and prints status when the operation succeeds.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero with a concrete error.

log_summary.py.description: Summarize one or more task logs into a compact diagnostics output file.

input.rule: Run from the repository root when producing diagnostics artifacts from logs or console output.

output.success: Writes or prints the requested diagnostics summary.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when log inputs are missing, unreadable, or summary writing fails.

migrate_ai_pipeline.py.description: Migrate an older AI pipeline installation to the current SocratexPipeline template and pack layout.

input.rule: Run from the repository root or an explicit target path when initializing, syncing, compiling, or migrating pipeline packages.

output.success: Completes the pipeline operation and writes, checks, or prints the requested pipeline artifact.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when inputs are invalid, target paths are unsafe, generated artifacts are stale, or pipeline checks fail.

normalize_documents.bat.description: Windows convenience wrapper for repository document normalization.

input.rule: Run from the repository root when checking or normalizing repository text files.

output.success: Completes text normalization, repair, write, or check work and prints OK or the generated output.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when files cannot be read/written or check mode finds stale normalization.

normalize_json_files.py.description: Internal JSON normalizer preserving repository formatting conventions.

input.rule: Run from the repository root when checking or normalizing repository text files.

output.success: Completes text normalization, repair, write, or check work and prints OK or the generated output.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when files cannot be read/written or check mode finds stale normalization.

normalize_markdown_emoji.py.description: Normalize markdown heading emoji markers, with check mode for task verification.

input.rule: Run from the repository root and prefer wrapper scripts over internal engines for normal document edits.

output.success: Completes the document operation or check and prints the requested result or OK status.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when paths, selectors, duplicate policy, schema rules, normalization, or post-edit checks fail.

normalize_text_files.py.description: Normalize repository text file line endings and final newlines with Python, including check mode support.

input.rule: Run from the repository root when checking or normalizing repository text files.

output.success: Prints OK when files are already normalized or lists normalized files.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when files cannot be read/written or check mode finds stale normalization.

open_pipeline_learning_issue.py.description: Open or print a GitHub issue request for pipeline learning discovered in a child project.

input.rule: Run from the repository root as part of task, git, learning, or repository health workflows.

output.success: Completes the repository workflow step and exits 0 when the requested operation succeeds.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when required inputs, repository state, external tooling, or workflow checks fail.

pipeline_bootstrap_index.py.description: Internal engine for building the pipeline bootstrap index from source document catalogs.

input.rule: Run from the repository root or an explicit target path when initializing, syncing, compiling, or migrating pipeline packages.

output.success: Completes the pipeline operation and writes, checks, or prints the requested pipeline artifact.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when inputs are invalid, target paths are unsafe, generated artifacts are stale, or pipeline checks fail.

pipeline_sweep.py.description: Run the cross-project SocratexPipeline sweep upgrade and smoke runner.

input.rule: Dry-run is default; -Execute is required to run update, smoke, or finalizer commands. -Project accepts NAME=PATH, source:NAME=PATH, or comma-separated specs.

output.success: Returns the per-project pass/fail/manual-attention summary.
output.failure: Throws when runtime resolution fails, config/project input is invalid, or the Python runner exits nonzero.

resolve_tool_runtime.py.description: Resolve the required Socratex Python runtime across Windows, Linux, and Steam Deck/Linux environments without hardcoded bundled interpreter paths.

input.rule: Use before Python-first pipeline tools when runtime availability is uncertain. Set SOCRATEX_PYTHON or RIFTBOUND_PYTHON to override discovery where supported.

output.success: Returns an executable Python 3 command path/source suitable for invocation.
output.failure: Throws with a concrete installation or environment-variable hint when no usable runtime is found.

resolve_workspace_root.py.description: Resolve the local Socratex workspace root from a workspace.json marker stored next to SocratexAI, without hardcoding work/projects/home paths.

input.rule: Use this for workspace-level operations such as sibling source checkout discovery, imports, archives, and drive exports. Project-local scripts should still resolve their own repo root relative to script location.

output.success: Prints the workspace root by default, or JSON with resolved workspace, projects, SocratexAI, tools, archive, drive export, and Drive URI fields when -Json is used.
output.failure: Throws when no workspace.json marker next to SocratexAI can be found or an explicit root is invalid.

rebuild_ai_compiled_context.py.description: Python generator for AI-compiled instruction files, SQLite knowledge database, and JSON knowledge-file fallback from source documents.

input.rule: Use this as the Python-first compiled context refresh/check path; keep the legacy wrapper only until the Python-only deletion pass.

output.success: Refreshes or checks generated AI-compiled artifacts and prints OK when complete.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when source compilation, knowledge compilation, or check mode fails.

reinitialize_pipeline.py.description: Reinitialize a project pipeline package from current source templates and selected packs.

input.rule: Run from the repository root or an explicit target path when initializing, syncing, compiling, or migrating pipeline packages.

output.success: Completes the pipeline operation and writes, checks, or prints the requested pipeline artifact.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when inputs are invalid, target paths are unsafe, generated artifacts are stale, or pipeline checks fail.

remove_pipeline.py.description: Remove SocratexPipeline files from a target project with dry-run and restore options.

input.rule: Run from the repository root or an explicit target path when initializing, syncing, compiling, or migrating pipeline packages.

output.success: Completes the pipeline operation and writes, checks, or prints the requested pipeline artifact.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when inputs are invalid, target paths are unsafe, generated artifacts are stale, or pipeline checks fail.

repair_mojibake.py.description: Repair common mojibake text corruption in selected files with Python, including check mode support.

input.rule: Run from the repository root when checking or repairing repository text files.

output.success: Prints OK when no repairs are needed or lists repaired files.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when files cannot be read/written or check mode finds needed repairs.

report_pipeline_learning.py.description: Generate a pipeline learning report from changed child-project artifacts.

input.rule: Run from the repository root as part of task, git, learning, or repository health workflows.

output.success: Completes the repository workflow step and exits 0 when the requested operation succeeds.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when required inputs, repository state, external tooling, or workflow checks fail.

run_quality_fix.py.description: Run repository quality fix steps that can safely repair generated or normalized artifacts.

input.rule: Run from the repository root as part of task verification or quality repair workflows.

output.success: Completes the quality fix/check operation and exits 0 when selected work succeeds.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when selected quality repair or verification cannot complete.

run_quality_gate.py.description: Python-first repository quality gate entrypoint that runs QUALITY-GATE.json, a named command subset, an explicit command, or standard project fallback tests.

input.rule: Use this as the canonical quality gate entrypoint for Python-first finalizers. Prefer --command-names for focused QUALITY-GATE.json subsets and --command only for explicit ad hoc commands.

output.success: Runs the selected quality command or contract and exits 0 when all selected checks pass.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when the explicit command, QUALITY-GATE command, or detected fallback test command fails.

run_quality_gate_contract.py.description: Python-first executor for the repository QUALITY-GATE.json command contract.

input.rule: Run without arguments from the repository root to execute QUALITY-GATE.json. Pass --command-names with comma-separated or repeated keys to run a focused subset.

output.success: Runs every selected quality-gate command and exits 0 after printing OK.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when the contract is missing, a command key is unknown, command text is empty, or any selected command exits nonzero.

set_directives.py.description: Apply, merge, or replace top-level directive files in a target pipeline installation.

input.rule: Run from the repository root or an explicit target path when initializing, syncing, compiling, or migrating pipeline packages.

output.success: Completes the pipeline operation and writes, checks, or prints the requested pipeline artifact.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when inputs are invalid, target paths are unsafe, generated artifacts are stale, or pipeline checks fail.

sync_pipeline_featurelist.py.description: Python-first instance pipeline_featurelist.json synchronizer from source feature manifests.

input.rule: Run after installed package updates so child projects can compare their local feature set against the source feature list.

output.success: Writes or prints the instance feature list and reports same/missing/extra counts.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when the source feature list is missing, empty, or invalid.

task_snapshot.py.description: Python-first compact task snapshot for repository finalization workflows.

input.rule: Use during Pass 0S as the Python replacement for task snapshot reporting.

output.success: Prints branch, last commit, status, diff summaries, untracked files, and known task document line counts.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when repository inspection fails.

task_flow_audit.py.description: Python-first task-flow closure checklist and conditional complex-task adversarial review prompt.

input.rule: Use during Pass 0S as the Python replacement for task_flow_audit.py; pass --project-root when invoking a managed package copy from a child project.

output.success: Prints required closure artifacts, latest changelog evidence when present, complex-task adversarial review prompts when applicable, and validates closure evidence when required.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when git or changelog parsing fails, required evidence is missing, or evidence lacks required closure fields.

update_code_line_index.py.description: Refresh or check docs-tech/CODE_LINE_INDEX.json and docs-tech/LARGE_FILES.json through Python.

input.rule: Use during Pass 0S as the Python replacement for code line index refresh. Use --changed-only for normal task closure and --check to fail on stale indexes.

output.success: Writes or verifies the code line index and generated large-file document, then prints indexed file counts.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when git/path discovery fails or check mode finds stale generated indexes.

update_pipeline_from_link.py.description: Python-first updater for installed SocratexAI packages from local path, zip URL, or shallow Git source.

input.rule: Default mode is cheap: managed package mirror, featurelist sync, directive refresh, and Python feature-contract check. Use --full-verify only for heavier post-update verification.

output.success: Mirrors managed SocratexAI paths, preserves project-owned files, runs cheap checks, and reports skipped full verification unless --full-verify was used.
output.failure: Exits nonzero on unresolved source, unsafe sync, failed directive update, failed feature-contract check, or failed full verification.

upgrade_from_reference_project.py.description: Upgrade a target project from a reference project pipeline snapshot.

input.rule: Run from the repository root or an explicit target path when initializing, syncing, compiling, or migrating pipeline packages.

output.success: Completes the pipeline operation and writes, checks, or prints the requested pipeline artifact.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when inputs are invalid, target paths are unsafe, generated artifacts are stale, or pipeline checks fail.

utf8_file_helpers.py.description: Provide reusable UTF-8 file read/write helpers for Python scripts.

input.rule: Import from Python text utilities instead of duplicating UTF-8 and line-ending logic.

output.success: Provides helper functions for UTF-8 reads, writes, and line-ending conversion.
output.failure: Raises Python exceptions for invalid line-ending modes or file IO errors.

run_interactive_setup.py.description: Run the interactive setup flow or dry-run it for verification.

input.rule: Run from the repository root or setup target when checking host/runtime setup.

output.success: Completes setup probing or configuration and prints the resulting setup status.
output.failure: Throws or exits nonzero when runtime detection or requested setup configuration fails.

write_utf8_file.py.description: Write text through the repository UTF-8 helper with line-ending and newline controls using Python.

input.rule: Use --value for simple values and stdin for multiline content.

output.success: Writes the target file as UTF-8 without BOM and prints OK.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when the target cannot be written.

sync_managed_pipeline_package.py.description: Python-first managed SocratexAI package mirror for source-owned package paths inside child projects.

input.rule: Use only for source-owned SocratexAI package paths; project memory and configuration outside SocratexAI remain child-owned.

output.success: Writes package manifest, reports preserved local overrides, and prints OK when complete.
output.failure: Exits nonzero on unsafe paths, missing profile files, invalid manifests, or copy/remove failures.

read_compiled_context.py.description: Read a source compiled instruction file or a compact source context document by alias with Python-only tooling.

input.rule: Prefer this Python reader for Python-only source context reads during the runtime retirement pass.

output.success: Compiled or source context content by default, or JSON with selected path and content when --json is used.
output.failure: Exits nonzero when no alias/path matches or when a compiled output hash is stale.

tool_error_log.py.description: Append a tool invocation or result-contract failure to docs-tech/TOOL-ERRORS.json, defaulting to the parent project registry when running from an installed SocratexAI package.

input.rule: Omit -Path for the default tool-error registry. Use -DetailsJsonFile for nested, multiline, or quote-heavy payloads; valid JSON is stored as structured data and invalid JSON is stored as text.

output.success: OK text or JSON with the inserted key and target path.
output.failure: Throws if the registry cannot be initialized or the entry cannot be appended.

tool_handler.py.description: Public single entry point for repository tool operations; validates inputs, normalizes parameters and aliases, delegates to the target script, logs input errors, and returns a stable result envelope.

input.rule: Use exactly one of -ParametersJson or -ParametersJsonFile. Prefer -ParametersJsonFile for nested, multiline, or quote-heavy parameter payloads.

output.success: Structured result envelope with ok=true, normalized operation, target script, normalized parameters, command, exit code, and captured output.
output.failure: Structured result envelope with ok=false, error type/message, and logged_error_key when validation logged an input failure.

project_design_context.py.description: Read project-specific code_design_required_reads from PIPELINE-CONFIG.json, print the project's design context, and write ignored/project_design_context_gate.json so downstream task checks can prove the design rules were loaded for the current HEAD.

input.rule: Run from the project root or pass -ProjectRoot. The script no-ops cleanly when code_design_required_reads is absent.

output.success: Writes ignored/project_design_context_gate.json with declared and loaded design-context files for HEAD.
output.failure: Throws when PIPELINE-CONFIG.json is missing or one of the declared design-context files cannot be read.

check_project_design_context_gate.py.description: Verify ignored/project_design_context_gate.json was refreshed for the current HEAD and changed code paths, so finalize/task-check flows can prove the project's design rules were loaded before code edits.

input.rule: Pass changed paths when checking a focused task; omit paths to derive them from git working tree + staged + untracked.

output.success: Confirms the gate file exists, matches current HEAD, and covers the changed code paths.
output.failure: Throws when the gate is missing, stale relative to HEAD or MaxAgeMinutes, or does not cover the declared design-context reads.

json_wrapper_entrypoints.py.description: Shared Python implementation for native JSON wrapper entrypoints.

input.rule: Internal module imported by json_*.py wrappers; use the wrapper scripts directly.

output.success: Routes wrapper arguments to json_list_doc.py with Python-only tooling.
output.failure: Raises or exits nonzero when wrapper arguments or JSON document operations fail.

context_loading.content_read: only when exact metadata for a specific script is needed

script_io_contract.purpose: Describe stable invocation contracts for scripts whose argument shape is not obvious from the script name alone.
script_io_contract.default_context_rule: The default SCRIPTS context remains name/index-only; read input/output only before invoking a script whose argument shape is not obvious.
script_io_contract.structured_json_rule: For long, multiline, nested, or quote-heavy JSON, prefer file/stdin value inputs over inline shell arguments. For order-only or structural JSON changes, use json_node_edit.py or narrower JSON wrappers instead of manual patching.

metadata.category_rule: Scripts live under tools/<category>/ and SCRIPTS.json keeps the filename index plus exact categorized path for execution.
